new_finding_this_run.detail: Prior state cited 'streak_gate(-15pts)' as a graduated rule applying a -15pt confidence penalty on restart. CORRECTED: This is risk/self_tuning.py CONSERVATIVE mode. Trigger: consecutive_loss_days >= 3 (lines 183-187 of self_tuning.py). With 4 consecutive losses at shutdown, self_tuning will activate CONSERVATIVE on restart. Effect: systemic reduction of max leverage and sizing thresholds globally — NOT a per-signal -15pt confidence deduction. The overall outcome is equivalent (fewer signals pass) but the mechanism differs. Important for: (1) soft-start planning — CONSERVATIVE mode may auto-reset faster than expected once winning trades accumulate; (2) rule interaction modeling — the deadlock is conf_floor_70_v1(-20pts) + CONSERVATIVE sizing, not +15pts penalty stacking.
new_finding_this_run.reason_not_auto_applied: Informational clarification — no graduated rule change needed. On restart, monitor self_tuning state in logs.

high_priority_findings.finding: STREAK_GATE_IS_SELF_TUNING (NEW THIS RUN): Prior state misidentified 'streak_gate(-15pts)' as a graduated rule. Actual mechanism: risk/self_tuning.py CONSERVATIVE mode (consecutive_loss_days >= 3). With 4 losses at shutdown, self_tuning activates CONSERVATIVE on restart — systemic effect on sizing/thresholds, not a -15pt per-signal penalty. Implication: CONSERVATIVE mode may clear faster once winning trades accumulate (resets consecutive_loss_days). The conf_floor_70_v1 fix remains critical but the interaction is systemic, not additive per-signal stacking.
